title: API rate limiting
tagline: Add per-IP and per-token sliding-window rate limits to protect the API from abuse.
risk: Low (additive middleware; no existing logic changed)
headline: The API currently has no rate limiting. A single client can issue unlimited requests. Adding a sliding-window limiter at the middleware layer protects against both accidental stampedes and intentional abuse, with 429 Too Many Requests + Retry-After responses.

decisions.help: Redis is durable and works across multiple API instances. In-memory is simpler but resets on restart and does not share state in a multi-process deploy.

options.label: Redis (Upstash or self-hosted) — durable, multi-instance safe (recommended)
In-memory — zero deps, fine for single-process / local dev
In-memory fallback when Redis unavailable; Redis in production

decisions.q: What is the rate limit key?
decisions.help: Per-IP limits protect against anonymous abuse. Per-token limits are fairer for authenticated multi-tenant APIs.

options.label: Both: per-IP for unauthenticated routes, per-token for authenticated routes (recommended)
Per-IP only (simpler, less fair for NAT users)

decisions.q: Default quota for authenticated API tokens
decisions.help: Start conservative; quotas can be raised per-tenant without a deployment.

options.label: 1 000 req / min per token (recommended starting point)
